Class RabbitMQConfig

java.lang.Object
com.bank.core.infrastructure.config.RabbitMQConfig
@Configuration public class RabbitMQConfig extends Object
Spring configuration for all RabbitMQ topology and listener infrastructure.

Declares the following AMQP topology:

 [banking.events] ──routing─key──▶ [notification.queue]    ──DLX──▶ [notification.queue.dlq]
 (DirectExchange)  ──routing─key──▶ [fraud-check.queue]     ──DLX──▶ [fraud-check.queue.dlq]

Each primary queue is backed by a Dead Letter Queue (DLQ). If a listener exhausts its configured retry budget (3 attempts with exponential back-off), the message is automatically forwarded to the corresponding DLQ for manual inspection.

Since:
1.0.0
Version:
1.0.0
Author:
Core Banking Team

    private static final String
    DLQ_SUFFIX
    DLQ suffix appended to each primary queue name.

    DLX_NAME
    Dead-letter exchange name (reuses the default exchange routing by queue name).

    org.springframework.amqp.core.DirectExchange
    bankingEventsExchange()
    Declares the primary banking.events DirectExchange.

    Declares the dead-letter exchange used to route failed messages to DLQs.

    Binds fraud-check.queue to the primary exchange using the transaction-completed routing key.

    Declares the fraud-check.queue.dlq for failed fraud-check messages.

    Binds fraud-check.queue.dlq to the DLX.

    fraudCheckQueue()
    Declares the fraud-check.queue with a DLX configuration.

    Binds notification.queue to the primary exchange using the transaction-completed routing key.

    Declares the notification.queue.dlq for failed notification messages.

    Binds notification.queue.dlq to the DLX.

    notificationQueue()
    Declares the notification.queue with a DLX configuration.
    org.springframework.amqp.rabbit.core.RabbitTemplate
    rabbitTemplate(org.springframework.amqp.rabbit.connection.ConnectionFactory connectionFactory)
    Configures the RabbitTemplate to use JSON message conversion.
    org.springframework.amqp.rabbit.config.SimpleRabbitListenerContainerFactory
    retryableContainerFactory(org.springframework.amqp.rabbit.connection.ConnectionFactory connectionFactory)
    Configures a SimpleRabbitListenerContainerFactory with a stateless retry interceptor (3 attempts, exponential back-off starting at 1 second).

  • Field Details

    • exchangeName

      @Value("${banking.rabbitmq.exchange}") private String exchangeName

    • notificationQueue

      @Value("${banking.rabbitmq.queue.notification}") private String notificationQueue

    • fraudCheckQueue

      @Value("${banking.rabbitmq.queue.fraud-check}") private String fraudCheckQueue

    • transactionCompletedKey

      @Value("${banking.rabbitmq.routing-key.transaction-completed}") private String transactionCompletedKey

      DLQ suffix appended to each primary queue name.

  • Method Details

    • bankingEventsExchange

      @Bean public org.springframework.amqp.core.DirectExchange bankingEventsExchange()
      Declares the primary banking.events DirectExchange.
      Returns:
      the configured exchange

    • deadLetterExchange

      @Bean public org.springframework.amqp.core.DirectExchange deadLetterExchange()
      Declares the dead-letter exchange used to route failed messages to DLQs.
      Returns:
      the dead-letter exchange

    • notificationQueue

      @Bean public org.springframework.amqp.core.Queue notificationQueue()
      Declares the notification.queue with a DLX configuration.
      Returns:
      the notification queue

    • notificationDlq

      @Bean public org.springframework.amqp.core.Queue notificationDlq()
      Declares the notification.queue.dlq for failed notification messages.
      Returns:
      the notification dead-letter queue

    • fraudCheckQueue

      @Bean public org.springframework.amqp.core.Queue fraudCheckQueue()
      Declares the fraud-check.queue with a DLX configuration.
      Returns:
      the fraud-check queue

    • fraudCheckDlq

      @Bean public org.springframework.amqp.core.Queue fraudCheckDlq()
      Declares the fraud-check.queue.dlq for failed fraud-check messages.
      Returns:
      the fraud-check dead-letter queue

    • notificationBinding

      @Bean public org.springframework.amqp.core.Binding notificationBinding()
      Binds notification.queue to the primary exchange using the transaction-completed routing key.
      Returns:
      the binding

    • fraudCheckBinding

      @Bean public org.springframework.amqp.core.Binding fraudCheckBinding()
      Binds fraud-check.queue to the primary exchange using the transaction-completed routing key.
      Returns:
      the binding

    • notificationDlqBinding

      @Bean public org.springframework.amqp.core.Binding notificationDlqBinding()
      Binds notification.queue.dlq to the DLX.
      Returns:
      the DLQ binding

    • fraudCheckDlqBinding

      @Bean public org.springframework.amqp.core.Binding fraudCheckDlqBinding()
      Binds fraud-check.queue.dlq to the DLX.
      Returns:
      the DLQ binding

    • retryableContainerFactory

      @Bean public org.springframework.amqp.rabbit.config.SimpleRabbitListenerContainerFactory retryableContainerFactory(org.springframework.amqp.rabbit.connection.ConnectionFactory connectionFactory)
      Configures a SimpleRabbitListenerContainerFactory with a stateless retry interceptor (3 attempts, exponential back-off starting at 1 second).

      After exhausting all retries, the message is nack'd without re-queue, triggering the DLX routing configured on each primary queue.

      Parameters:
      connectionFactory - the AMQP connection factory
      Returns:
      the retryable listener container factory

    • rabbitTemplate

      @Bean public org.springframework.amqp.rabbit.core.RabbitTemplate rabbitTemplate(org.springframework.amqp.rabbit.connection.ConnectionFactory connectionFactory)
      Configures the RabbitTemplate to use JSON message conversion.
      Parameters:
      connectionFactory - the AMQP connection factory
      Returns:
      the configured template
